metadata.title: Hakim Technical Debt Ledger Example Dataset
metadata.description: Synthetic illustrative dataset demonstrating the Hakim technical-debt schema. Entries are not claims about this repository.
metadata.evidence_requirement: A live repository debt claim must reference files that exist in the target repository and include commit, pull request, issue, or operator evidence.

entries.description: Custom cache class with TTL instead of functools.lru_cache
entries.justification: Needed distributed caching with Redis backend for future multi-instance deployment. Current usage is single-instance only. Ladder rung 3 (StdLib) bypassed for speculative scalability that may never materialize. Decision driven by architecture team's preference for 'enterprise-grade' solutions without profiling data.
entries.removal_plan: Replace custom APICache class (src/utils/cache.py, 89 lines) with @lru_cache(maxsize=1000) decorator on fetch_api_data function. If distributed cache needed later, add Redis adapter as separate module without changing call sites. Trigger: when actual multi-instance deployment is scheduled.

entries.description: Custom date picker component instead of native <input type='date'>
entries.justification: UX team insisted on branded date picker with custom styling and range selection. Ladder rung 4 (Native Platform) bypassed for aesthetic preferences over functional requirements. Native HTML5 date input provides 100% accessibility and zero bundle size.
entries.removal_plan: Replace DatePicker.jsx (156 lines + 12KB CSS) with <input type='date'> styled via CSS custom properties. For range selection, use two native date inputs with min/max constraints. Trigger: when UX team accepts native styling or when bundle size becomes critical.

entries.description: Pydantic models for API request validation instead of manual dataclass validation
entries.justification: Team familiar with Pydantic from previous projects. Ladder rung 3 (StdLib) bypassed for developer convenience over stdlib dataclasses. Manual validation would add ~30 lines but eliminate dependency and reduce cold start time by 200ms.
entries.removal_plan: Replace Pydantic models (src/models/*.py, 234 lines total) with @dataclass + manual validate_* functions. Use typing module for type hints. Trigger: when cold start time becomes SLA-critical or when Pydantic introduces breaking changes.

entries.description: Celery task queue for background email sending instead of asyncio + multiprocessing
entries.justification: Anticipated need for complex task scheduling, retries, and monitoring dashboard. Ladder rung 3 (StdLib) bypassed for infrastructure that doesn't exist yet. Current email volume is 50/day, easily handled by asyncio. Celery adds Redis dependency and 2 worker processes.
entries.removal_plan: Replace Celery tasks (src/tasks/email.py, 67 lines) with asyncio.Queue + multiprocessing.Pool. Use stdlib logging for monitoring. Add Celery only when: (1) email volume > 10,000/day, OR (2) complex scheduling required, OR (3) retry logic exceeds 50 lines. Trigger: metrics-based, not speculative.

entries.description: SQLAlchemy ORM for simple CRUD operations instead of raw SQL with sqlite3
entries.justification: Team preference for ORM abstraction and migration tooling (Alembic). Ladder rung 3 (StdLib) bypassed for developer productivity. Current schema has 4 tables with simple relationships. Raw SQL would be more transparent and faster for simple queries.
entries.removal_plan: Replace SQLAlchemy models (src/db/models.py, 189 lines) with dataclasses + sqlite3 raw SQL queries. Use Alembic-style migration scripts manually. Trigger: when query performance becomes bottleneck OR when team size > 5 developers (abstraction becomes valuable).

entries.description: requests library for HTTP client instead of urllib.request
entries.justification: Initial project setup before Hakim skill adoption. Ladder rung 3 (StdLib) bypassed for convenience. urllib.request provides same functionality with zero dependencies.
entries.removal_plan: Replace requests.get/post calls (src/api/client.py, 34 lines) with urllib.request.urlopen + json.loads. Add 15-line helper function for common patterns. Trigger: during next refactoring sprint.

usage_instructions.interpretation: This bundled file is a synthetic example dataset. Do not treat any entry, path, timestamp, resolution status, or statistic as a fact about the Hakim repository.
usage_instructions.live_claim_rule: Before promoting an entry to a live repository claim, verify every related file and attach commit, pull request, issue, or operator evidence.
usage_instructions.adding_entry: Use /hakim-debt command or manually append to entries array
usage_instructions.resolving_entry: Set resolved_at to ISO timestamp, update actual_loc_saved
usage_instructions.querying: Use jq: cat technical_debt_ledger.json | jq '.entries[] | select(.severity=="high")'
usage_instructions.reporting: Use /hakim-gain command to generate summary report
